works.title: Episode 1: Rapid Response Hotline
works.description: President Trump returned to power promising the largest deportation operation in American history. The episode puts the first months of that policy push in context and examines community-run Rapid Response Hotlines that monitor ICE activity and dispatch legal aid.

works.title: Episode 2: Los Angeles and Fire
works.description: After the January 7 fires across Los Angeles, this episode looks at California fire from two angles: the role of Indigenous fire stewardship in modern fire management and an audio portrait about growing up with Santa Ana winds.

works.title: Episode 3: A Home by Any Other Name
works.description: A three-part look at homelessness and displacement across the Bay Area: an I-80 encampment in Berkeley, an RV community in San Francisco, and a group living on boats off the coast of Sausalito.

works.title: Episode 4: MDMA & The Bay
works.description: An exploration of MDMA's Bay Area roots, from early therapeutic use through federal scheduling and today's research debates, including the FDA's rejection of a recent MDMA-assisted therapy application.

title: The Protocol
description: A six-part podcast exploring the story of medical treatment for transgender young people - how the care began, the lives it changed, and the legal and political fights that could end it in the United States.
On June 18, 2025, The Supreme Court upheld a Tennessee ban on transgender care for minors. That decision allows similar bans to continue in more than 20 other states.
And it comes as the Trump administration is targeting the care - threatening to go after the hospitals and doctors who continue to provide it around the country.
"The Protocol" is the story of this care - where it came from, who it was meant to help, and how legal and political fights in the U.S. came to endanger it.

works.title: Episode One: The Beginning
works.description: The story of youth gender medicine starts in The Netherlands, with a clinical psychologist and a 16 year-old who was determined to go through life as the gender he long felt he was.

works.title: Episode Two: The Gender Kids
works.description: The new treatment is codified into a protocol, and the Dutch publish a landmark study that launches the field of youth gender medicine. A former patient describes the benefit of early treatment in a society that is still hostile to trans people.

works.title: Episode Three: The American Approach
works.description: In Boston, a psychologist helps set up the first youth gender clinic in the U.S., adapting the Dutch approach. Across the country, a rising star in the field questions their methods. Meanwhile, demand increases worldwide as the types of patients seeking care change.

works.title: Episode Four: The Whistleblower
works.description: A small Midwestern gender clinic gets consumed by a growing political fight when a whistleblower goes public. She had begun to see the treatments as harmful, and decided the political route was the only way to fix it.

works.title: Episode Five: The Review
works.description: The heated debate is not limited to the U.S. In England, a pediatrician tasked with figuring out how the country's health care system should provide the care finds that the evidence is "remarkably weak." One major medical group argues that's missing the point - and repeating history.

works.title: Episode Six: The Now
works.description: State bans are enacted. The Supreme Court is poised to weigh in. President Trump declares that there are only two genders and threatens to withhold federal funds from hospitals that provide pediatric gender treatments. We hear from kids, parents and providers caught in the middle about the complicated decisions they are making.

works.title: The Implications for the Failed Wagner Rebellion

title: Mixtape
description: Before the podcast and the smartphone, there was the cassette tape and the Walkman - recordable, rewritable, spliceable, compact, and mobile tools that changed how people moved through the world and listened to voices speaking directly to them.
In five episodes from around the world, Mixtape explores how the cassette brought people together, pulled them apart, and reshaped culture in lasting ways.

works.title: Mixtape: Dakou
works.description: In late-1990s China, damaged scraps of Western music known as dakou tapes helped transform the musical landscape. The episode follows the historians, critics, and musicians who reimagined rock and roll through those fragments.

works.title: Mixtape: Jack and Bing
works.description: As Bing Crosby's influence started to fade, he turned to recording innovations rooted in wartime technology. The story traces how that shift changed media and audience habits for decades.

works.title: Mixtape: The Wandering Soul
works.description: During the Vietnam War, U.S. military psychological operations weaponized a Vietnamese ghost story using tape-based audio tactics. The episode explores the campaign and the long afterlife of those sounds.

works.title: Mixtape: Cassetternet
works.description: Experiments in the early 1980s explored broadcasting software over radio so listeners could record it on cassettes, creating analog information networks that disrupted institutions and foreshadowed modern social systems.

works.title: Mixtape: Help?
works.description: A three-part finale: how cassettes fueled the self-help industry, how taped history traveled across Africa with Lost Boys, and how old mixtapes still carry emotional weight as personal artifacts.
